(Rooted Updated Tree) Testing Phylogenetic Signal in Phenotypic Data

Spencer Toth

2026-02-12

library(tidyverse)
library(phylosignal)
library(broom)
library(rstatix)
library(adephylo)
library(ape)
library(phylobase)
library(formattable)
library(corrplot)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(cowplot)
library(ggpubr)
library(grid)
library(gridGraphics)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr) 
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
library(phytools)
`%!in%` <- negate(`%in%`)

Prepare data for analysis

Import phenotype and phylogenetic tree data

# Import phenotypic data

# Growth Rates
growth_rate_data_file <- read_csv("../formatted_phenotype_data/growth_rate.csv")

# Import phylogenetic tree 
#tr <- read.tree("/Users/spencertoth/Desktop/research_ongoing/gardnerella_phylogenetic_signal/gardnerella_pathogenic_phenotypes_1_17_2026/RAxML_bestTree.gardTree")
#tr <- read.tree("/Users/spencertoth/Desktop/research_ongoing/gardnerella_phylogenetic_signal/unrootedGardPhylogeny_withOutgroup.tre")
#tr_rooted <- root(tr, outgroup = "QUERY___B_longum_51A", resolve.root = TRUE)
#tr <- tr_rooted
tr <- read.tree("/Users/spencertoth/Desktop/research_ongoing/gardnerella_phylogenetic_signal/rootedGardPhylogeny_noOutgroup.tre")
is.rooted(tr)
## [1] TRUE
# Rename tree nodes to match strain names in strain-phenotype data 
numeric_tips <- tr$tip.label[tr$tip.label %in% c("101", "5-1")]
tr$tip.label[match(numeric_tips, tr$tip.label)] <- paste0("Gv", numeric_tips)
tr$tip.label[tr$tip.label == "14018"] <- "S14018"
tip_labels <- tr$tip.label
is.rooted(tr)
## [1] TRUE

Average the phenotype values across technical replicates per strain

growth_rate_data <- growth_rate_data_file %>%
  mutate(Strain = as.character(Strain)) %>%
  group_by(Strain) %>% summarise(mean_mSlope = mean(mSlope, na.rm = TRUE))
gr_vector <- setNames(growth_rate_data$mean_mSlope, growth_rate_data$Strain)
gr_range <- range(gr_vector, na.rm = TRUE)

Rename complete-numeric strains

# Strain 14018 not present in: pb_vector, sf_vector (did not pass QC for these experiments)
vecs <- list(gr=gr_vector)
vecs[] <- lapply(vecs, function(v) {
  idx <- names(v) == "14018"   
  if(any(idx)) names(v)[idx] <- "S14018"   
  v
})

({gr_vector<<-vecs$gr})
##    S14018     315-A       AMD   C0011E4   C0040C2   C0056B5   C0084H9   C0093B3 
## 0.9054030 0.7717042 0.5854646 0.8287432 0.5139235 0.3413151 0.5796882 0.6969131 
##   C0096A1   C0100B2   C0101A1   C0102A2   C0179B4  CMW7778B     Gv101     Gv5-1 
## 0.6234009 0.7491596 0.5703236 0.8379834 0.7429103 0.6351465 0.3907391 0.6154267 
##   JCP7275  JCP8017B   JCP8066   JCP8108     UM224      UM35 
## 0.5650313 0.8517620 0.8050377 0.6271462 0.7657100 0.6607661
names(gr_vector)
##  [1] "S14018"   "315-A"    "AMD"      "C0011E4"  "C0040C2"  "C0056B5" 
##  [7] "C0084H9"  "C0093B3"  "C0096A1"  "C0100B2"  "C0101A1"  "C0102A2" 
## [13] "C0179B4"  "CMW7778B" "Gv101"    "Gv5-1"    "JCP7275"  "JCP8017B"
## [19] "JCP8066"  "JCP8108"  "UM224"    "UM35"

Update: “phylosignal” R-package used to conduct phylogenetic signal tests (Moran’s I and Pagel’s lambda)

Subset phylogenetic tree to the strains with phenotype information

# Phenotypes with all 22 strains: carrying capacity, growth rate and minimum pH
data_tips <- intersect(names(gr_vector), tr$tip.label)   
tr_subset <- keep.tip(tr, data_tips)
tr_subset$root.edge <- 0.05 
tip_labels <- tr_subset$tip.label
n_strains <- length(tip_labels) # 22
is.rooted(tr_subset)
## [1] TRUE
# Function to get root-to-tip distances for all tips
get_root_to_tip <- function(tree) {
  # For a rooted tree, the root is usually node Ntip + 1
  root_node <- Ntip(tree) + 1
  
  # All pairwise node distances
  D <- dist.nodes(tree)
  
  # Tips are rows 1..Ntip
  tip_indices <- 1:Ntip(tree)
  root_to_tip <- D[tip_indices, root_node]
  
  # Return as a data frame
  data.frame(
    tip = tree$tip.label,
    root_to_tip_distance = as.numeric(root_to_tip),
    row.names = NULL
  )
}

root_to_tip_table <- get_root_to_tip(tr_subset)
print(root_to_tip_table)
##         tip root_to_tip_distance
## 1   C0179B4            0.1236920
## 2   C0056B5            0.1480142
## 3     Gv101            0.1539509
## 4   C0100B2            0.1510372
## 5       AMD            0.1775258
## 6   C0096A1            0.1787596
## 7   C0102A2            0.1829598
## 8     Gv5-1            0.1818464
## 9     UM224            0.1914767
## 10 CMW7778B            0.5984910
## 11  C0101A1            0.5335876
## 12  C0093B3            0.5148959
## 13  JCP8066            0.5168688
## 14     UM35            0.5133911
## 15  C0040C2            0.5305496
## 16 JCP8017B            0.5322402
## 17  JCP7275            0.4776537
## 18  C0084H9            0.4754711
## 19  JCP8108            0.5146526
## 20    315-A            0.4791123
## 21  C0011E4            0.4786119
## 22   S14018            0.4816770
p <- ggplot(root_to_tip_table, aes(x = root_to_tip_distance)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 22, fill = "steelblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Root-to-Tip Path Distances",
    x = "Distance to Root",
    y = "Count",
    subtitle = paste("N =", nrow(root_to_tip_table), "tips")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

print(p)

# Summary stats
summary(root_to_tip_table$root_to_tip_distance)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##  0.1237  0.1795  0.4781  0.3698  0.5148  0.5985

Moran’s I and Pagel’s Lambda: Phylogenetic Signal along BM Gradient

# Distribution of Test Statistic as percentage of BM increases
set.seed(42)

plot(tr_subset, type="phylogram", root.edge=0.1)

is.rooted(tr_subset)
phylosim <- phyloSim(tree = tr_subset, methods = c("I", "Lambda"), model = "BM", pb = FALSE)
plot(phylosim, stacked.methods = FALSE, quantiles = c(0, 1), ylab = "Phylogenetic Signal Value")

plot.phylosim(phylosim, what = "pval", stacked.methods = TRUE)

mean(phylosim$sim.stat[1, 1, ])  # I at 0% BM 
## [1] -0.04356091
mean(phylosim$sim.stat[1, 2, ])   # Lambda at 0% BM 
## [1] 0.1323256
is.rooted(tr_subset)
## [1] TRUE
set.seed(42)
# Simulated traits along Brownian Motion given phylogenetic tree 
bm_raw <- rTraitCont(tr_subset, model = "BM")
# Normalize to 0-1 range for comparison with growth rate data 
bm <- (bm_raw - min(bm_raw)) / (max(bm_raw) - min(bm_raw))

# Random generated trait dataset 
random_raw <- rnorm(n_strains)
# Scaled to 0-1 range for comparison with growth rate data
random <- (random_raw - min(random_raw)) / (max(random_raw) - min(random_raw))

dat_growth_rate <- data.frame(
  growth_rate = gr_vector[match(tip_labels, names(gr_vector))],   
  random      = random,
  bm          = bm
  
)
rownames(dat_growth_rate) <- tip_labels
p4d_growth_rate <- phylo4d(tr_subset, dat_growth_rate)
# Trait Distribution
barplot.phylo4d(p4d_growth_rate, tree.type = "phylo", tree.ladderize = TRUE, center = FALSE, scale = FALSE)

# Z-scores 
barplot.phylo4d(p4d_growth_rate, tree.type = "phylo", tree.ladderize = TRUE)

phyloSignal(p4d = p4d_growth_rate, methods = c("I", "Lambda", "C"))
## $stat
##                   Cmean           I       Lambda
## growth_rate  0.16877553  0.06241527 4.789203e-01
## random      -0.03845959 -0.09270568 7.574327e-05
## bm           0.46759790  0.36710921 1.055237e+00
## 
## $pvalue
##             Cmean     I    Lambda
## growth_rate 0.074 0.135 0.3993158
## random      0.438 0.706 1.0000000
## bm          0.001 0.001 0.0010000
# Phylogenetic correlogram (uses Local Moran's I autocorrelation)
growth_rate.crlg <- phyloCorrelogram(p4d_growth_rate, trait = "growth_rate")
random.crlg <- phyloCorrelogram(p4d_growth_rate, trait = "random")
bm.crlg <- phyloCorrelogram(p4d_growth_rate, trait = "bm")

plot(bm.crlg, main = "Brownian Motion Simulation (Strong Signal)")

plot(random.crlg, main = "Random Trait (No Signal)")

plot(growth_rate.crlg, main = "Phenotypic Trait: Growth Rate")

# Local Indicator of Phylogenetic Association (LIPA)
growth_rate.lipa <- lipaMoran(p4d_growth_rate)
growth_rate.lipa.p4d <- lipaMoran(p4d_growth_rate, as.p4d = TRUE)

barplot.phylo4d(p4d_growth_rate, bar.col = (growth_rate.lipa$p.value < 0.05) + 1, tree.type = "phylo", tree.ladderize = TRUE, center = FALSE, scale = FALSE)

barplot.phylo4d(growth_rate.lipa.p4d, bar.col = (growth_rate.lipa$p.value < 0.05) + 1, tree.type = "phylo", tree.ladderize = TRUE, center = FALSE, scale = FALSE)

Perform permutation test on Pagel’s Lambda statistic

# Extract tree + observed and simulated traits  
tree <- as(p4d_growth_rate, "phylo")
trait_data <- p4d_growth_rate@data  # (growth_rate, random, bm)
trait_names <- colnames(trait_data)

# Number of permutations
n_perm <- 1000
set.seed(42)

# Store results for all traits
results <- list()

# Loop through each trait
for(j in 1:ncol(trait_data)) {
  trait <- trait_data[[j]]
  trait_name <- trait_names[j]
  
  cat("\n---", trait_name, "---\n")
  
 # Pagel's λ  
  obs_lambda <- phylosig(tree, trait, method = "lambda")$lambda
  
  # Generate null distribution using 1000 permutations of trait data
  lambda_null <- numeric(n_perm)
  for(i in 1:n_perm) {
    shuffled_trait <- sample(trait)
    lambda_null[i] <- phylosig(tree, shuffled_trait, method = "lambda")$lambda
  }
  
  # right-tailed (We reject H₀ if observed λ is unexpectedly large i.e. right tail)
  p_val <- mean(lambda_null >= obs_lambda)
  
  results[[trait_name]] <- list(
    obs_lambda = obs_lambda,
    p_value = p_val,
    null_dist = lambda_null
  )
  
  cat("Observed λ =", round(obs_lambda, 4), "\n")
  cat("p-value =", round(p_val, 4), "\n")
}
# Plot null distribution 
par(mfrow=c(1,3), mar=c(4,4,2,1))
for(trait_name in names(results)) {
  res <- results[[trait_name]]
  dens <- density(res$null_dist)
  
  plot(dens, main=trait_name, xlab="λ", lwd=2, 
       xlim=c(0,1), ylim=c(0,max(dens$y)*1.2))
  abline(v=res$obs_lambda, col="red", lwd=3, lty=2)
  
  # Shade area beyond observed
  x_tail <- seq(res$obs_lambda, 1, len=100)
  y_tail <- approx(dens$x, dens$y, xout=x_tail)$y
  polygon(c(res$obs_lambda, x_tail, 1), c(0, y_tail, 0), 
          col=rgb(1,0,0,0.3), border=NA)
}

# Print Results 
results_df <- data.frame(
  trait = trait_names,
  lambda = sapply(results, function(x) round(x$obs_lambda, 4)),
  p_value = sapply(results, function(x) round(x$p_value, 4))
)
print(results_df)
##                   trait lambda p_value
## growth_rate growth_rate 0.4789   0.125
## random           random 0.0001   1.000
## bm                   bm 0.9999   0.004

(Ultrametric Test) Forced-Ultrametric Tree –> Simulated Brownian-Motion (BM) model (Phylogeny with 22 strains)

# Distribution of Test Statistic as percentage of BM increases
set.seed(42)
tr_subset_ultra <- force.ultrametric(tr_subset, method = "nnls") # nnls or extend
plot(tr_subset_ultra)

tr_subset_ultra$edge.length[tr_subset_ultra$edge.length <= 0] <- 1e-6
phylosim <- phyloSim(tree = tr_subset_ultra, methods = c("I", "Lambda"), model = "BM", pb = FALSE)
mean(phylosim$sim.stat[1, 1, ])  # I at 0% BM 
## [1] -0.03759388
mean(phylosim$sim.stat[1, 2, ])   # Lambda at 0% BM 
## [1] 0.04719917
plot(phylosim, stacked.methods = FALSE, quantiles = c(0, 1), ylab = "Phylogenetic Signal Value")

plot.phylosim(phylosim, what = "pval", stacked.methods = TRUE)

set.seed(42)
# Simulated traits along Brownian Motion given phylogenetic tree 
bm_raw <- rTraitCont(tr_subset_ultra, model = "BM")
# Normalize to 0-1 range for comparison with growth rate data 
bm <- (bm_raw - min(bm_raw)) / (max(bm_raw) - min(bm_raw))

dat_growth_rate <- data.frame(
  growth_rate = gr_vector[match(tip_labels, names(gr_vector))],   
  random      = random,
  bm          = bm
  
)
rownames(dat_growth_rate) <- tip_labels
p4d_growth_rate <- phylo4d(tr_subset_ultra, dat_growth_rate)
# Trait Distribution
barplot.phylo4d(p4d_growth_rate, tree.type = "phylo", tree.ladderize = TRUE, center = FALSE, scale = FALSE)

# Z-scores 
barplot.phylo4d(p4d_growth_rate, tree.type = "phylo", tree.ladderize = TRUE)

phyloSignal(p4d = p4d_growth_rate, methods = c("I", "Lambda"))
## $stat
##                      I       Lambda
## growth_rate  0.0982733 6.761806e-05
## random      -0.0813243 6.761806e-05
## bm           0.5187202 1.013739e+00
## 
## $pvalue
##                 I Lambda
## growth_rate 0.077  1.000
## random      0.638  1.000
## bm          0.001  0.001
growth_rate.crlg <- phyloCorrelogram(p4d_growth_rate, trait = "growth_rate")
random.crlg <- phyloCorrelogram(p4d_growth_rate, trait = "random")
bm.crlg <- phyloCorrelogram(p4d_growth_rate, trait = "bm")

plot(bm.crlg, main = "Brownian Motion Simulation (Strong Signal)")

plot(random.crlg, main = "Random Trait (No Signal)")

plot(growth_rate.crlg, main = "Phenotypic Trait: Growth Rate")

Perform permutation test on Pagel’s Lambda statistic

# Extract tree + observed and simulated traits  
tree <- as(p4d_growth_rate, "phylo")
trait_data <- p4d_growth_rate@data  # (growth_rate, random, bm)
trait_names <- colnames(trait_data)

# Number of permutations
n_perm <- 1000
set.seed(42)

# Store results for all traits
results <- list()

# Loop through each trait
for(j in 1:ncol(trait_data)) {
  trait <- trait_data[[j]]
  trait_name <- trait_names[j]
  
  cat("\n---", trait_name, "---\n")
  
 # Pagel's λ  
  obs_lambda <- phylosig(tree, trait, method = "lambda")$lambda
  
  # Generate null distribution using 1000 permutations of trait data
  lambda_null <- numeric(n_perm)
  for(i in 1:n_perm) {
    shuffled_trait <- sample(trait)
    lambda_null[i] <- phylosig(tree, shuffled_trait, method = "lambda")$lambda
  }
  
  # right-tailed (We reject H₀ if observed λ is unexpectedly large i.e. right tail)
  p_val <- mean(lambda_null >= obs_lambda)
  
  results[[trait_name]] <- list(
    obs_lambda = obs_lambda,
    p_value = p_val,
    null_dist = lambda_null
  )
  
  cat("Observed λ =", round(obs_lambda, 4), "\n")
  cat("p-value =", round(p_val, 4), "\n")
}
# Plot null distribution 
par(mfrow=c(1,3), mar=c(4,4,2,1))
for(trait_name in names(results)) {
  res <- results[[trait_name]]
  dens <- density(res$null_dist)
  
  plot(dens, main=trait_name, xlab="λ", lwd=2, 
       xlim=c(0,1), ylim=c(0,max(dens$y)*1.2))
  abline(v=res$obs_lambda, col="red", lwd=3, lty=2)
  
  # Shade area beyond observed
  x_tail <- seq(res$obs_lambda, 1, len=100)
  y_tail <- approx(dens$x, dens$y, xout=x_tail)$y
  polygon(c(res$obs_lambda, x_tail, 1), c(0, y_tail, 0), 
          col=rgb(1,0,0,0.3), border=NA)
}

# Print Results 
results_df <- data.frame(
  trait = trait_names,
  lambda = sapply(results, function(x) round(x$obs_lambda, 4)),
  p_value = sapply(results, function(x) round(x$p_value, 4))
)
print(results_df)
##                   trait lambda p_value
## growth_rate growth_rate 0.0001   1.000
## random           random 0.0001   1.000
## bm                   bm 0.9999   0.001
